name: Kappa Theta Pi
summary: Kappa Theta Pi is a co-ed professional technology fraternity at the University of Maryland. KTP is open to all students with an interest in technology, regardless of major. Students can rush and pledge each fall and spring semester to become a brother.

name: Young Democratic Socialists of America
summary: The Young Democratic Socialists of America is part of the international struggle to build a better world. We organize college campuses to fight for the immediate needs of workers and students, while building our capacity to fight for structural changes.

name: Alpha Chi Sigma
summary: We are a professional, co-ed fraternity! Our brothers participate in many networking events with our professional chapters, and on campus provide services such as free tutoring for intro chemistry classes and lab equipment sales for new students.

name: Kappa Omega Alpha Policy Professional Fraternity Gamma Chapter
summary: The purpose of this Fraternity shall be to acknowledge students in pursuit of their interests in the field of public policy on both a social and professional scale; to uphold the strength of fraternalism; to strengthen the ideals of Kappa Omega Alpha.

name: Leatherbacks Combat Robotics
summary: We build and compete fighting robots

name: Technica Hacks
summary: Technica is the world’s largest hackathon for underrepresented genders, hosted every fall.

name: Office of Sustainability​
summary: The University of Maryland Office of Sustainability supports and advances environmental performance, economic prosperity and social equality through a variety of initiatives. The staff facilitate the development and implementation of sustainable polici...

name: CIVICUS
summary: CIVICUS Living and Learning Program

name: Smith Finance Group
summary: To establish a collaborative finance community at Smith; a community which empowers students interested in finance across all learning levels and backgrounds with the knowledge and expertise to distinguish themselves in competitive finance fields.

name: One for the World
summary: One for the World is a student organization that encourages undergraduates and graduates to give a percentage of their future annual income to one of our many charities. Our mission is to end extreme poverty through the idea of effective altruism.

name: LCSL - Alternative Breaks
summary: The University of Maryland Alternative Breaks program engages students in short-term service-learning immersion experiences that address social issues in local, national, and international communities.

name: Mechanical and Reliability Engineering Graduate Student Association
summary: The Mechanical and Reliability Engineering Graduate Student Association (MEGA) at the University of Maryland, College Park seeks to improve the student experience for graduate students in ME/RE.

name: Epsilon Eta
summary: A coed professional evnironmental science fraternity.

name: Society For Biomaterials University of Maryland College Park Student Chapter
summary: The Society for Biomaterials (SFB) chapter at UMD is a student-led organization through the BIOE department. We connect students across engineering and life sciences through workshops, networking events, and outreach to the national SFB community.

name: Foundational Learning and Mentorship Experience
summary: FLAME empowers the next generation of STEM leaders by supporting the academic, social, and emotional development of students from underrepresented groups through experiment-based after-school mentorship programs.

name: Eta Sigma Gamma National Health Education Honorary - Epsilon Chapter
summary: Eta Sigma Gamma is the only National Professional Honorary in Health Education. Since being revitalized in 2017, the Epsilon Chapter of Eta Sigma Gamma (ESG) has proudly excelled in the areas of service, academics, and membership growth. Through our ph...

name: Stamp Human Resources
summary: The Adele H. Stamp Student Union has approximately 100 full-time graduate assistants, professional staff, and an estimated 400 student employee members working in 12 units.

name: Maryland Association of Black Journalists
summary: MABJ is a student professional organization for students working toward degrees in journalism at the University of Maryland who will work together under the auspice of the NABJ in the education of its members with respect to the journalism field.

name: Nu Rho Psi: National Honor Society in Neuroscience
summary: Nu Rho Psi is UMD's National Neuroscience Honor Society. Our goal as a society is to engage in meaningful and nuanced conversations of neuroscience and its vast implications. Students can engage with professionals in the field to explore their curiosity.

name: Graduate Science Policy at UMD
summary: SPG cultivates opportunities for UMD students to support and advocate for evidence-based policies. We aim to connect scientific research to decision-making processes at the local, state, and federal levels of the U.S. government.

name: The Science Café at College Park
summary: Join us for Coffee, Refreshments, and a Book discussion! An environment where all students, especially STEM majors, in particular, can further broaden their scope of education through various forms of literature.

name: Terps After Dark
summary: Terps After Dark

name: UMD Student Affairs
summary: The Vice President’s Office oversees the activities of the departments and coordinates division-wide initiatives and management practices.

name: Association for Women in Computing
summary: Association for Women in Computing aims to create a supportive community for minorities in computing majors across campus through social & professional events.

name: Alpha Rho Chi - Olynthius
summary: Alpha Rho Chi (APX) is the National Professional Co-Ed Fraternity for Architecture and the Allied Arts. UMD's Olynthius chapter hosts events that further the professional interests of members as well as events that donate proceeds to various charities.

name: Maryland Science Cafe
summary: Join us for Coffee, Refreshments, and a Book discussion! An environment where all students, especially STEM majors, in particular, can further broaden their scope of education through various forms of literature.

name: Maryland Night Live
summary: Maryland Night Live is a comedy production that brings the best comedians and musicians at UMD together in a Saturday Night Live-styled format. Our goal is to push the university's comedic potential to heightened levels and aspire for comedic excellence.

name: Smith Investment Fund
summary: Smith Investment Fund (SIF) is a non-profit firm founded in 2018, run by students at the University of Maryland – College Park. We invest with two separate strategies - discretionary and systematic - on behalf of our advisors and partners.

name: Banneker Key Community Council
summary: The Banneker Key Community Council (BKCC) is a student-run organization that provides social, professional, and service-oriented programming to Banneker Key Scholars!

name: American Society of Heating, Refrigerating and Air-Conditioning Engineers
summary: ASHRAE UMD Student Chapter aims to promote education, leadership and industrial partnerships for educating members in the field of heat transfer and HVAC/R (heating, ventilating, air-conditioning, and refrigerating).

name: Balloon Payload Program at the University of Maryland
summary: BPP provides students with the opportunity to design and fly scientific and engineering payloads on high-altitude balloons to 80,000-100,000 feet.

name: Migrating Shells
summary: Migrating Shells seeks to provide a holistic space for students with an immigrant identity or experience, foster a community of support and well-being, and mobilize around immigrant rights on campus and surrounding communities.

name: WiCyS Student Chapter at the University of Maryland
summary: WiCyS is dedicated to bringing together women in cybersecurity from academia, research and industry to share knowledge, experience, networking and mentoring

name: Snider Consulting Group
summary: We work with undergraduate University of Maryland clubs and small College Park organizations to determine best practices for their groups. SCG works with them to grow.

name: Asian American Studies Program
summary: The Asian American Studies Program at UMD focuses on the lives, history, and culture of Asian Americans from a comparative point of view. Specifically, we study persons who have immigrant and ancestral ties to any region of Asia and the Pacific.

name: Reed-Yorke Health Professions Advising Office
summary: Our office advises students interested in the health professions. prehealth.umd.edu

name: Department of Behavioral and Community Health
summary: Behavioral and community health focuses on individual and community lifestyle behaviors and aspects of our social environment that affect our health.

name: Department of Resident Life
summary: Great experiences at great universities are the product of applying knowledge and learning/growing within a dynamic, culturally diverse, and rigorous academic community. Resident Life is here to support those pursuits while living on campus.

name: Maryland Smith Graduate Admissions - MBA, MS, and Plus 1 programs
summary: The Maryland Smith Graduate Admissions Office works with prospective undergraduate students on navigating the pathway to Graduate School.

name: College of Education, Office of Student Services
summary: The College of Education offers multiple program areas of specialization and degree options, including combined 5-year Bachelor's and Master's degree programs, as well as double major and minor programs.

name: Lean On Me College Park
summary: Lean On Me College Park aims to provide UMD students with non-crisis, peer-to-peer text-based emotional support anytime, anywhere. We also hope to promote mental health advocacy and awareness on campus through partnerships and events.

name: University of Maryland Libraries
summary: The University Libraries are your libraries. Our faculty and staff are here to help you access the academic resources and develop the research strategies you need to succeed.

name: Center for Global Migration Studies
summary: The Center for Global Migration Studies is UMD's interdisciplinary hub for teaching and research on immigration.

name: History Department
summary: The University of Maryland Department of History offers courses on peoples, cultures, and societies from the beginning of recorded time to present. For more information and a list of course offerings visit: http://history.umd.edu

name: LSAMP STEM
summary: LSAMP STEM is an organization dedicated to providing professional development resources and a community for students majoring in STEM.

name: Belonging & Community at UMD
summary: We work to cultivate well-being, belonging, and a vibrant learning and working community for all.

name: Empowering Women in Law UMD
summary: A gender-inclusive group focused on encouraging women to enter the legal field and work towards underrepresented positions of power. Members can meet with women in law, work on fundraising for women's higher education, and even facilitate LSAT prep.

name: Women in Economics
summary: WIE is dedicated to providing a professional and social support system for women and non-binary individuals with an interest in economics and their supporters.

name: Tikkun Olam Makers
summary: We are a community dedicated to designing, creating, and providing solutions to the unmet needs and challenges of those in need. "Tikkun Olam" means "to heal the world" - and we hope to do our part to heal the world around us.

name: BigTh!nk AI
summary: The purpose of this student organization is to bring awareness and appreciation towards the growing interdisciplinary field of artificial intelligence. We intend to advance our members’ knowledge and experience of our members through meeting and projects

name: Nyumburu Cultural Center
summary: Nyumburu Cultural Center promotes an understanding of and appreciation for African-American Culture in all of its richness and complexity.

name: Individual Studies Program
summary: IVSP allows students to combine courses to create a unique, interdisciplinary major that fits with their interests, passions, and future goals.

name: Department of Plant Science and Landscape Architecture
summary: Would you like to contribute to a sustainable future and enhanced environmental quality through design, conservation and management of plants and other natural resources, and the planning, design and management of landscapes?

name: LGBTQ+ Equity Center
summary: We envision the University of Maryland as a fully equitable community that empowers innovators and agents of social justice for lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender, and queer people.

name: The CARing Project and En Camino mentoring programs
summary: he CARing Project, En Camino, and stART programs are three weekly tutoring and mentoring programs (Tu/Wed/Thurs) where UMD students are paired with local elementary school students (digitally for Fall 2020) to help them learn and grow.

name: Project Global Officer for Persian and Arabic Studies
summary: Project Global Officer is a Department of Defense initiative that promotes critical language education opportunities through language study for ROTC students.

name: Federal and Global Fellows Program
summary: The program combines a fall seminar course taught by industry experts with a spring internship. The program prepares students to excel in professional internships and pursue careers of influence and impact, especially in public service.

name: The Dingman Center for Entrepreneurship
summary: The Dingman Center is one of the nation’s pre-eminent institutions where the research, education and practice of entrepreneurship are pursued vigorously.

name: Black Pre-Law Association at the University of Maryland
summary: The Black Pre-Law Association at the University of Maryland is an organization that strives to create a supportive and encouraging community for Black pre-law students.

name: Singh Sandbox Makerspace
summary: Sandbox is a student-run makerspace for artists, designers, and hackers (or anyone wanting to learn) to make things to create amazing things. All UMD students, staff, and faculty are welcome. Come in with your ideas and build your projects.

name: Hack4Impact-UMD
summary: Hack4Impact-UMD is an organization focused on creating software for social good. We partner with nonprofit groups nationwide to help them with creating web applications, websites, and more.

name: InfoSci FI
summary: InfoSci FI (Female-Identifying) is a tech-focused STEM organization dedicated to supporting female-identifying students in developing their professional presence and enhancing their connections with professors, tech professionals, and the community.

name: Punchbowl Comedy
summary: Punchbowl’s purpose is to provide aspiring stand up comedians multiple opportunities to perform in front of an audience and to help improve their comedic writing abilities. We want to create a more accessible “comedy scene” on campus.

name: Student Planning Association at the University of Maryland
summary: The Student Planning Association is an organization of students working toward a Master of Community Planning (M.C.P.) degree. Its primary function is to promote student involvement in the continued development of the planning program and profession.

name: LACS Graduate Collective
summary: The Latin American Studies Center Graduate Collective is an interdisciplinary space for Latinx, Latin American, Caribbean, Latin-Americanist, and Caribbeanist graduate students to collaborate and engage with other graduates from across campus.

name: University Honors Student Board
summary: UHSB serves as an advisory board to the directorate of the University Honors program. We foster a positive and productive relationship between UH students and faculty, helping shape the goals and values of our program.

name: NMDP at University of Maryland
summary:
NMDP is an organization that is dedicated to recruiting stem cell donors for patients with various blood cancers and blood disorders, collecting donations for the organization, and raising awareness for these diseases.

https://bethematch.org/

name: First Year Book Program
summary: Each year since 1993, the First Year Book Program has selected a book for the first-year community to read with the goal of creating a shared intellectual experience and the opportunity to discuss the book from a variety of disciplinary perspectives.

name: Engineering Graduate Student Society
summary: The mission of this organization is to promote the graduate student experiences in the Clark school by voicing student concerns, ideas, and goals with the Dean and foster collaboration among all engineering departments.

name: Office of Student Conduct
summary: The Office of Student Conduct works to make campus safe by educating students, investigating referrals, and holding our community accountable

name: Extended Studies
summary: Further your education, build credits towards a degree, enrich your professional portfolio, prepare for college, or expand your knowledge. Discover flexible learning opportunities to Keep Learning.

name: Paints and Shells
summary: A chill club for art enthusiasts, hobbyists, and professionals to meet other artists on campus and participate in fun drawing challenges, events, and more. Join our Discord server to hear about our events and meetings: https://discord.gg/mzjU4DP

name: Division of IT Classroom Support
summary: Our department provides support and care for the tech needs of the 300+ classrooms on campus.

name: Naval ROTC at UMD (NROTC MIDSHIPMAN ASSOC)
summary: Naval ROTC program to commission officers into the Navy and Marine Corps

name: Division of Information Technology
summary: The Division of IT plans, develops, supports, and maintains computing, networking, and telecommunications services to empower UMD community members’ teaching, scholarship, and full engagement in our community and the world.

name: Minor in International Development and Conflict Management
summary: MIDCM is an undergraduate program of instruction designed for students who are interested in enhancing their understanding of the theory and practice of international development, conflict resolution, and humanitarian relief.

name: Department of Environmental Science and Technology
summary: As human populations continue to grow, so do concerns about the effects of humans on ecosystems and, in turn, how ecosystems and the built environment affect human health and well-being. The ENST undergraduate major trains students in not only understand

name: IEEE EPS
summary: The IEEE Electronics Packaging Society is the leading international forum for scientists and engineers engaged in the research, design and development of revolutionary advances in microsystems packaging and manufacturing.

name: Science in the Evening Non-Traditional Student Society
summary: SIENTSS is a student organization for post-baccalaureate students currently enrolled in the Science in the Evening (SIE) program.

name: Google Developer Student Club
summary: The Google Developer Student Club at UMD is a community that provides an educational and creative environment for undergraduate students interested in various technologies and programming languages.

name: NeuroTech@UMD
summary: NeuroTech@UMD provides students with resources and opportunities to explore neurotechnologies, such as brain-computer interfaces (BCIs), through hands-on workshops, projects, competitions, industry connections, and more!

name: A. James Clark School of Engineering
summary: The A. James Clark School of Engineering houses 9 engineering majors, engineering student affairs, and academic support programs

name: International Student Council
summary: The International Student Council (ISC), formerly known as the International Student Advisory Board (ISAB), consist of a group of students who serve as a liaison between international students and ISSS to ameliorat

name: TerraPeers
summary: To connect new students with fellow Terps and the broader UMD community to foster a greater sense of belonging and enthusiasm for engagement at UMD.

name: Chemical and Biomolecular Engineering Graduate Student Association
summary: The goal of CGA is to build community, connections, and friendships among graduate students, while also establishing a line of communication between students and the faculty in chemical and biomolecular engineering.

name: Student Academy of Audiology
summary: UMD's chapter of the Student Academy of Audiology exists to serve as a collective voice for the rights, interests, and welfare of students pursuing careers in audiology.

name: Apex Fund
summary:
Apex Fund is a fundamental, long-only student equity fund. Apex is managed by UMD students seeking to invest in strong, FCF generative businesses with market caps under 20 billion.

Visit our website: https://apexfund.github.io/apex-web

name: Undergraduate Quantum Association
summary: UMD's only organization for undergraduates interested in the fields of quantum science and technology! We provide a space for students passionate or interested in learning more about quantum physics, computing, technology, information science etc.

name: Break Through Tech DC at UMD
summary: The University of Maryland announced on March 2nd, 2021 a grant from Break Through Tech to propel more women into computing degrees and careers in tech—through curriculum innovation, career access and community building.

name: The Office of Civil Rights and Sexual Misconduct
summary: The Office of Civil Rights and Sexual Misconduct (OCRSM) administers the University of Maryland's non-discrimination policies and works to foster a learning and working environment free from all forms of discrimination and harassment.

name: Moot Court
summary: The Moot Court program allows undergraduate students to develop understandings of constitutional law by participating in simulations of appellate advocacy before a mock Supreme Court.

name: Partners In Health Engage at UMD
summary: Partners In Health (PIH) Engage is a dynamic organization dedicated to promoting health as a human right. We educate our community, advocate for policies, and generate resources to make this right reality for those living in poverty.

name: Future Mathematics Teachers' Council
summary: We gather to discuss relevant issues in teaching and learning of mathematics, participate in statewide efforts related to future math teachers, and share experiences with other math educators.

name: App Dev Club
summary: App dev club provides an opportunity for students interested in mobile application development to immerse themselves in the field through educational workshops as well as collaborative student projects.

name: American Studies Undergraduate Association
summary: The AUA serves to improve the experience of American Studies (AMST) and U.S. Latina/o Studies (USLT) Minor students by promoting the department, hosting events, and developing relationships between students and professionals.

name: Science Competitions Advocating for Learning Equity
summary: The goal of our organization is to reduce inequity in Prince George's County elementary schools by encouraging meaningful competition between students that fosters a positive learning environment and simultaneously reinforces the PGCPS STEM curriculum.

name: The American Association for Aerosol Research (AAAR) Student Chapter- UMD
summary: The AAAR Student Chapter @ UMD is a student-run organization to foster community where students can exchange scientific ideas about aerosol research.

name: Formula EV at UMD
summary: UMD's Formula EV is a student organization in which a team of students fully design, build, and race an electric formula-style racecar powered each year. We compete in the Formula Hybrid and Electric (April) and Formula SAE EV (June) competitions yearly.

name: Department of Environmental Safety, Sustainability and Risk-Office of Risk Management
summary: Department of Environmental Safety, Sustainability and Risk (ESSR) is the department charged with the oversight of health and safety for the University of Maryland. Within ESSR, the Office of Risk Management supports the academic

name: Black Girls Vote
summary: Our mission is to inspire women of color to use the political process and improve the quality of life for their families and the collective community. Our objective is to engage, educate and empower women to activate their voice by utilizing their vote.

name: The Petey Greene Program at UMD
summary: The Petey Greene Program at UMD serves as the campus chapter for the national nonprofit organization, The Petey Greene Program. Our mission is to support the academic goals of incarcerated and formerly incarcerated people through tutoring and advocacy.

name: The John Quincy Adams Society
summary: JQASUMD is a student organization committed to identifying, educating, and equipping the next generation of scholars and policy leaders to encourage a new era of realism and restraint in American foreign policy by hosting events to educate our peers.

name: Terps Racing Formula IC
summary: Terps Racing Formula IC is a student organization in which students fully design, build, and race a formula-style race car powered by an internal combustion engine each year.

name: Black Scholars in Biology
summary: The Black Scholars in Biology (BSIB) is a professional network of Black graduate students in the Biological Sciences at the University of Maryland, College Park (UMCP).

name: Coalition for Human Rights in the Philippines at the University of Maryland
summary: We are campus community members in solidarity with Filipino people's advancement of human rights and genuine, lasting peace in the Philippines. All people, including those not of Filipino ancestry, are welcome to join.

name: The Optica Student Chapter at UMD
summary: The Optica Student Chapter at UMD is the community for UMD students, pursuing fields related to optics and photonics to come together and cultivate a thriving environment for advancing and expanding the field of science through Optica, at UMD and beyond.

name: fart club
summary: fart club

name: Reentry Redefined
summary: This organization is committed to helping solve the United State's issue of high recidivism rates and make reentry back into society easier for incarcerated individuals.

name: American Water Works Association Chesapeake Section University of Maryland Student Chapter
summary: The purpose of this organization is to promote the common professional interests of its members. Membership is open to all regularly-enrolled students at the University of Maryland who are interested in the water industry.

name: International Business Student Organization
summary: IBSO presents an opportunity for students to learn about International Business from other passionate individuals, connect with professionals and alumni, and expand on their knowledge of business beyond our borders.

name: Amazon Web Services Cloud Club at the University of Maryland
summary:
This club is conducted to spread knowledge on Amazon Web Services technologies and inform students on the implications of cloud computing in all careers and how they can learn it for their benefit.

Discord: https://discord.gg/NKqxVDqEMA

name: Kappa Delta Pi
summary: Kappa Delta Pi is an international educational honor society for future professionals in the field of education. We inspire professionals to thrive and build a community to create lifelong opportunities and provide equitable education for all.

name: Carillon Communities
summary: Carillon Communities is a one-year living-learning program where first year students work with faculty to ask Big Questions that matter to our world, and learn to use teamwork and creative problem solving approaches.

name: Accessibility and Disability Service
summary:
The Accessibility and Disability Service (ADS) office works with undergraduate and graduate students to determine academic, housing, and student employment accommodations
Through the variety of services offered, we work to ensure that your accommodation

name: The Blood Pressure Screening Project
summary: We are dedicated to improving the health and well-being of our local community by tackling the issue of high blood pressure. High blood pressure is a major risk factor for heart disease, which is a leading cause of death in our community.

name: Trials of Color at the University of Maryland
summary: We work to bridge the gap between underserved cancer patients and their healthcare providers through advocacy, education, and fundraising. By supporting underprivileged minority populations, we aim to promote inclusivity and diversity in cancer research.

name: Human Development Student Association
summary: The HD Student Association is for those interested in Human Development. Students will be provided career development resources while also fostering a sense of community within the HD department.

name: Med-ucate
summary: Med-ucate strives for health awareness in MD & DC through student-led workshops, resources, and impactful community roles, fostering an enriched educational journey.

name: Black Alliance Network
summary: Uniting UMD's black student organizations into a powerful network for advocacy, collaboration, and cultural celebration, fostering a united community.

name: Audubon Student Chapter at the University of Maryland
summary: The purpose of this Student Chapter is to protect birds and the places they need, today and tomorrow through bird walks, conservation projects, citizen science, and advocacy.

name: Adobe Design Club
summary: We are a club associated with Adobe Student Ambassadors and provide UMD students with access as well as opportunities for Adobe related events, activities, project, etc.

name: Diamondback Innovators
summary: Diamondback Innovators is a student-run, engineering design and consulting organization, dedicated to teaching members of the organization how to design and create cost-efficient, high-quality mechanical solutions for its clients.

name: Latino Pre-Law Society
summary: The Latino Pre-Law Society is dedicated to cultivating a dynamic community that empowers Latino students aspiring to pursue careers in law. Our mission is to foster academic excellence, professional development, and a strong sense of identity.

name: The Student Chapter of the American Society for Microbiology at University of Maryland
summary: This is the University's student chapter of ASM

name: Black and Latinx in STEM
summary: BLIS is dedicated to nurturing the academic and social growth of black and brown students in the STEM field. This organization is committed to celebrating and acknowledging the accomplishments of black and brown individuals in STEM at UMD.

name: The Sociology Collective
summary: Are you looking for ways to engage with sociology? Join The Sociology Collective, an undergraduate organization run by SOCY students for SOCY students, and all others interested in the field! We plan movie nights, outings, fundraisers, and service events

name: Billiards at University of Maryland
summary: A club committed to fostering a healthy billiards community at the University of Maryland. Whether that means providing already experienced players opportunities to shine or teaching newer players the game, the billiards club is committed to aid students

name: First-Generation Student Association
summary: The First-Generation Student Association aims to provide a supportive community and resources for First Generation students at UMD through both educational and social events.

name: Graduate Students with Disabilities
summary: This group is intended to be a private, confidential, accessible, and safe space for graduate students to chat and get to know each other. We currently use Whatsapp. We aim to recognize wholeness, and commit ourselves to collective access and liberation.

name: Women's Surgical Society at The University of Maryland
summary: The Women's Surgical Society at The University of Maryland is dedicated to providing an environment where women feel empowered and welcome within the field of medicine. We encourage our members to bridge the gap between gender discrimination in medicine.

name: Warrior Canine Connection Club
summary: A club where we raise and train service dogs for veterans and also educate and support the importance of service dogs for our veterans.

name: Undergraduate Law Review at University of Maryland
summary: This is an undergraduate organization aiming at aiding students in publishing articles on law topics under the mentorship of legal scholars.

name: Maryland Quantitative Finance Club
summary: MQFC explores finance, math, and tech through workshops, networking, and projects, preparing members for future careers in both tech & finance. Open to all, we turn complex concepts into accessible knowledge, preparing students for the real world!

name: Arab American Association of Engineers and Architects at the University of Maryland
summary: AAAEA-UMD works to create a strong sense of community within the College Park campus that will facilitate growth both professionally and personally. We plan to hold events that will help our members become more prepared for their future careers.

name: FBLA at UMD
summary: Sister organization to DECA at UMD, aimed at instilling self reliance and entrepreneurship in all interested UMD students.

name: BSE Scholars Club
summary: The BSE Scholars Club is an open-access organization dedicated to extending the BSE Scholars academic program's premier experiential learning, professional development, and community service opportunities to all University of Maryland undergraduates.

name: Business Beyond Borders
summary: Business Beyond Borders is a dynamic organization created to help solve issues of opportunity scarcity and educational gaps amongst students. Our core mission is to be a catalyst for change, creating invaluable business opportunities for minorities.

name: Israeli American Council (IAC) - Mishelanu
summary: Mishelanu works to bring the spirit and culture of Israel to campus. The program provides students with an Israeli home on campus and builds a bridge between Israel and the campus community.

name: BioE Graduate Student Society
summary: The Bioengineering Graduate Student Society (BGSS) is a student organization within the Fischell Department of Bioengineering at the University of Maryland, College Park.

name: Biocomputational Engineering Club
summary: The Biocomputational Engineering Club is an organization for all majors interested in the intersection of Computer and Natural Sciences. The club hosts programming workshops, seminars, biotech networking events, and information on biotech opportunities.

name: Integrated Life Sciences Community Leadership Council
summary: The Integrated Life Sciences Community Leadership Council (ILS CLC) is a group of students from the Integrated Life Sciences student body with the goal of empowering and uniting ILS students.

name: Fashion Business Association
summary: As a Fashion Business Association, we empower fashion industry professionals and companies through advocacy, education, and networking opportunities, enhancing their market presence and business capabilities.

name: Nepali Graduate Students Association
summary: The organization is dedicated to nurturing camaraderie among graduate students of Nepali descent. Its primary mission is to enhance academic and scientific cooperation and to celebrate the rich cultural heritage of Nepal.

name: Cultoure Magazine
summary: Cultoure Magazine strives to bring light to all the creativity and individuality this POC community has. We will do so by having publications promoting small business owners, artists, designers, photographers, writers, models, and other organizations.

name: Smith Equity Research
summary: Smith Equity Research aims to teach students about equity research, how to conduct fundamental analysis of companies with Bloomberg Terminals, and how to put together a report putting all your findings together.

name: Health-Link Society
summary: We are a service-oriented organization committed to fostering unity and mentorship among the next generation of pre-health students, all within the spirit of volunteerism.

name: Makerspace For All Club
summary: The Makerspace For All Club promotes and supports access for Makerspace technologies and trainings for all undergraduate students, regardless of financial ability, at the University of Maryland, College Park.

name: Women in Electrical and Computer Engineering
summary: Women in Electrical and Computer Engineering hosts regular social, professional, and service-based events. Our main goal is to encourage recruitment and retention in the ECE dept. and to build a friendly and supportive community. All genders are welcome!

name: The UnLocked Project
summary: The UnLocked Project supports children of incarcerated parents in the DMV through peer support sessions, a resource hub, and awareness events. It fosters community, leadership, and advocacy while raising awareness about the challenges these children face

name: Legal Guardian Angels
summary: Legal Guardian Angels (LGA) is a student organization at the University of Maryland dedicated to advocating for children and youth. Members engage in volunteer work with local nonprofits, support community initiatives, and learn from guest speakers.

name: Kids Helping Kids
summary: Through a partnership with Johns Hopkins Pediatric Oncology, Kids Helping Kids raises awareness and funds for pediatric oncology research.

name: Blockchain at Maryland
summary: Blockchain at Maryland is a student-led organization to advance research, development and application of Blockchain technology at UMCP. We want to position UMCP as a leader in blockchain research and innovation.

name: Space Sciences Outreach Cooperative
summary: We aim to establish a centralized outlet at University of Maryland for space sciences outreach in order to better and more efficiently connect with our local communities.

name: Phi Beta Sigma Fraternity Inc. (Epsilon Psi Chapter)
summary: Epsilon Psi Chapter of Phi Beta Sigma at UMD champions brotherhood, scholarship, and service. They focus on community service, educational initiatives, and leadership development, aiming to uplift their community and promote social change.

name: Habesha's In Tech
summary: The club helps its members grow professionally and personally by offering mentorship, networking opportunities, and professional development activities. It also gives members access to industry experts' advice and experiences.

name: Solar Gamera at the University of Maryland, College Park
summary: Solar Gamera (founded 2011) is a solar-powered rotorcraft designed to carry a person in hover. Manned solar-powered hover is a groundbreaking accomplishment which has never been documented before. Aspiring engineers, join to make history!

name: Playtime Project
summary: The mission of Playtime Project (Playtime) is to cultivate resilience in children experiencing family housing insecurity by providing and expanding access to transformative play experiences.

name: National Association of Hispanic Journalists
summary: The National Association of Hispanic Journalists (NAHJ) is an educational and charitable association dedicated to the recognition and professional advancement of Hispanic students, professionals and educators in the field of journalism.

name: Busily
summary: Busily is an all-about-DATA club for students interested in data science, visualization, and analytics. Members gain hands-on experience through data projects, workshops, and teamwork.

name: The Policy Hatch
summary: We are a student-led policy newspaper and research journal focusing on public policy issues and open to students of any major. We will be publishing op-eds, analysis pieces, satires, and letters to the editor, as well as longer-form research papers.

name: Pre-Health Social
summary: Pre-Health Social is a student group at UMD that connects future healthcare professionals through fun events, volunteer opportunities, and social meetups. Open to all pre-health tracks, join us to build community, grow professionally, and give back!

name: International Student Mentorship Program
summary: We're a group that provides international students workshops and resources to help them adjust to life at UMD and beyond.

name: Tank Enthusiast Club
summary: A club for students interested in tanks, military vehicles, and history. We explore their evolution, impact, and cultural relevance through trips, presentations, and more. We do not condone war or violence. Our focus is educational and technical.

name: The Lamin Organization
summary: The Lamin Organization (TLO) is a non-profit organization with the mission of providing assistive devices to children from families who cannot afford them, bridging caps The name is inspired by the founders cousin who lives with Sotos syndrome.

name: UFC Club
summary: UMD UFC Club brings together students who enjoy playing UFC 5 and watching live UFC fights. We meet weekly on Tuesdays or Wednesdays for gaming sessions and host watch parties during UFC Fight Nights. All experience levels are welcome!

name: The Black Business Association
summary: An organization dedicated to empowering black students to pursue their business ideas and goals. From obtaining internships to promoting small businesses, the Black Business Association is here to create a  community for Black students in business.

name: Baja SAE at the University of Maryland
summary: Baja SAE at the University of Maryland designs builds, and races off-road vehicles for SAE competitions, showcasing engineering innovation, teamwork, and multidisciplinary excellence

name: American Institute of Chemical Engineers at UMD
summary: Club for anyone and everyone crazy about chemical engineering!

name: Student Parent Club
summary: The Student Parent Club aims to foster a sense of community among undergraduate students who are parenting, as well as cultivate a sense of affinity. The club hosts events, and programming and other ways to engage.

name: Olympia Academy: Physics and Philosophy
summary: Academy Olympia is dedicated to discussions on the nature of reality. Our name refers to a group formed in 1903 by Einstein, Habicht, and Solovine who shared a love for math, physics, and philosophy. Meetings will include guest lecturers and debates.

name: Women's Investing Society
summary: Girls Who Invest is an organization that empowers members to gain knowledge and experience in the world of investing and finance for those who wish to pursue a career in those areas. We create opportunities for members to achieve their professional goals

name: Teal Titans
summary: Teal Titans at UMD is dedicated to saving lives through outreach and advocacy for the prevention and cure of ovarian cancer, while enhancing the quality of life for survivors and their caregivers.

name: Asian American Pre-Law Society
summary: The Asian American Pre-Law Society is committed to creating a strong community for Asian American students pursuing legal careers. We aim to create a network through professional development, peer connections, and educational opportunities.

name: The David C Driskell Center
summary: The Driskell Center is a creative incubator dedicated to a world where Black artists exist at its center. We invite inquiry, experimentation, and dialogue.

name: The Movement Street - University of Maryland Chapter
summary: The Movement Street transforms how America volunteers—blending service, creativity, and accessibility. Through national campaigns and local chapters, we make meaningful community impact achievable for everyone, everywhere.

name: Terps for Safe AI
summary: Have you ever worried about an AI takeover? Whether you're inexperienced or an AI enthusiast, Terps for Safe AI is a place for you to learn and discuss AI safety related research areas, and even get involved in cutting-edge research at UMD!

name: GEOH Student Organization
summary: The GEOH Student Organization is within the Department of Global, Environmental, and Occupational Health. The goal of the organization is to provide professional development and community building opportunities to students in the department.

name: Scientists for Public Research
summary: SPR’s purpose is to uplift the message and ideals of science that advances public knowledge. SPR was founded as a way to bridge the gap between scientists and civic/public life, an important intersection with a noticeable gap.

name: Electrical and Computer Engineering Graduate Student Association
summary: ECEGSA supports the academic, professional, and social interests of Electrical and Computer Engineering graduate students at UMD through events, advocacy, and community engagement.

name: Satellite Development Program
summary: SatDev supports nanosatellite missions at UMD.

name: CS Base - University of Maryland Chapter
summary: CS Base is a non profit whose mission is to raise accessibility and awareness of computer science education, especially in underserved communities. We typically organize classes, workshops, and camps for many different areas of computer science.

name: Kendo Club at the University of Maryland
summary: The purpose of the club is to practice and promote the traditional Japanese martial art known as "Kendo". Kendo means "the way of the sword", and is the traditional martial art that samurai used to train. Beginners to experienced members welcome!

name: Interdisciplinary Engineering Design Club
summary: IEDC seeks to address the deficit of aesthetic design considerations in modern engineering education by fostering collaboration between disciplines and encouraging creativity, thoughtfulness, and beauty in everyday engineering practice and education.

name: Society of Manufacturing Engineers at UMD
summary: SME at UMD promotes interest in manufacturing engineering through technical learning, professional development, and community engagement. We offer workshops, speakers, competitions, and facility tours to help students explore and grow in the field.

name: Maryland Scholars Investment Fund
summary: Maryland Scholars Investment Fund offers investing and finance learning opportunities to students through weekly lessons and management of a stock portfolio.

name: National Black Association for Speech- Language and Hearing
summary: We advance equity in speech, language, and hearing sciences by empowering underrepresented students, supporting research, and increasing access to resources and outreach that improve awareness and outcomes for diverse communities.

name: National Society of Black Women in Medicine at the University of Maryland
summary: NSBWM is committed to supporting current and future healthcare professionals and scientists in efforts to combat educational and professional racial inequities by promoting attainability through educational resources, unity, and mentorship.

name: Design, Build, Fly at the University of Maryland
summary: Join a team that designs, builds, and flies an RC airplane. We compete in AIAA's Design, Build, Fly competition and compete against other schools from around the world.

name: Girls Into VC at UMD
summary: The GIVC Club exists to empower female students to enter and excel in venture capital and entrepreneurship. We provide education, mentorship, networking opportunities, and hands-on experience in evaluating startups and understanding the VC ecosystem.

name: UMD Loop
summary: We make kickass engineers, and win competitions -- in that order!

name: PIXL (People, Interaction, and eXperience Lab)
summary: PIXL is an inclusive community for women and underrepresented communities at UMD interested in immersive technologies- including AR/VR, game development, HCI, and more.

name: ARHU Undergraduate Student Coalition
summary: The ARHU USC is an interdisciplinary hub for ARHU undergraduate students, enhancing the student experience through collaborative programming, centralized outreach, and logistical support to boost engagement across the University of Maryland as a whole.

name: ElevateU Organization
summary: ElevateU is a student-led organization dedicated to supporting incoming need-based students as they transition into college. We work to reduce financial barriers through donation drives, scholarships, and resource initiatives, grounded in the belief that

name: Woodworking 101
summary: Woodworking 101 is a club aimed at getting new people into the hobby of woodworking using the Terrapin Works Woodshop, a woodworking resource that is completely free to use for all students, staff, and faculty at the University of Maryland College Park.

name: Voices of Equity
summary: The goal of our chapter is to educate communities, inspire action, and empower impact.

name: Legally Speaking
summary: A safe space where everyone can share their thoughts on the legal world + a platform to publish legal writing. We discuss legal topics such as court cases, laws we agree/disagree with, controversial issues, and anything related to the legal system.
